summary:
Held 0 of today's 10 biggest UK/EU movers.

• 6 upside misses — TEP.PA +2%, EDV.L +2%, GLE.PA +2%, AIR.PA +2%, SAF.PA +2%. These cleared the universe screen but no strategy picked them up; the morning brief either filtered them on a catalyst/RSI/earnings rule or had nothing positive to trigger an entry.

• 4 downside moves (DBK.DE -2%, ENR.DE -2%, CPG.L -2%, BATS.L -2%) are unreachable on a long-only basket — no mechanism to express a short or pair-trade.

• Most instructive miss: TEP.PA (+2.47%) — Positive media sentiment shift: 'TP's Comeback Story is Starting to Look More Credible' piece (ABC Money, ~May 29) suggested the AI-disruption narrative is turning — no hard fundamental event on the day. Why we passed: Outside any active strategy's universe. TEP.PA is a large-cap French BPO/IT-services name (Euronext Paris) down ~24% YTD, likely below any momentum-entry threshold; the move was soft-catalyst (media narrative reversal) with no earnings or M&A trigger to flag pre-market.

top_movers.catalyst: Positive media sentiment shift: 'TP's Comeback Story is Starting to Look More Credible' piece (ABC Money, ~May 29) suggested the AI-disruption narrative is turning — no hard fundamental event on the day.
top_movers.miss_reason: Outside any active strategy's universe. TEP.PA is a large-cap French BPO/IT-services name (Euronext Paris) down ~24% YTD, likely below any momentum-entry threshold; the move was soft-catalyst (media narrative reversal) with no earnings or M&A trigger to flag pre-market.
top_movers.suggestion: Add a 'depressed-large-cap sentiment reversal' scan: names with 52-week rank <20% that appear in ≥2 positive media mentions within 24h trigger a watchlist alert — catches narrative-flip bounces without requiring a hard catalyst or changing universe membership.

top_movers.catalyst: Gold spot rose ~1% to ~$4,501/oz on May 28-29 amid inflation and Middle East risk sentiment; Endeavour Mining's ~2× gold-price beta amplified the move with no company-specific news on the day.
top_movers.miss_reason: Outside any active strategy's universe — EDV.L is an LSE-listed mid-cap gold miner likely excluded by sector or index-membership filters. Commodity-beta moves require pre-open positioning; intraday reactive signals arrive too late to capture the gap.
top_movers.suggestion: Add an LSE gold-miners sub-universe (gold-spot correlation ≥ 0.70, mkt-cap ≥ £1B) to the momentum strategy, armed on days when gold spot prior-session return exceeds +0.75%, to capture systematic precious-metals beta without broad sector expansion.

top_movers.catalyst: EUR 1.00 final dividend goes ex 2026-06-01 (T+3), driving pre-ex-date capture buying; May 27 AGM formally confirmed new Chairman William Connelly, removing a residual governance overhang.
top_movers.miss_reason: Outside any active strategy's universe — large-cap Euronext bank not in current sector scope. Move is classic dividend-capture positioning 3 days pre-ex-date; momentum signals only fire after the buying window has already opened.
top_movers.suggestion: Add a pre-ex-dividend scanner for Euronext large-cap financials: flag names within 3 sessions of ex-date on above-average volume as a temporary universe expansion, so capture setups are visible before the window closes.

top_movers.catalyst: Airbus announced a strategic AI partnership with Mistral AI on May 28 (post-close), granting full product-suite licences for commercial, defence and space applications; market continued reacting into the May 29 session.
top_movers.miss_reason: Outside any active strategy's universe. AIR.PA is a CAC 40 mega-cap (~€140B) aerospace/defence name; catalyst was a soft partnership announcement with no hard revenue figure, making pre-move signals weak and the name ineligible for any standard momentum or mean-reversion screen.
top_movers.suggestion: Add an AI-partnership event trigger to the news-reactive universe: if headline contains 'AI partnership' AND name is CAC 40 / AEX constituent, admit to the catalyst strategy for the following session with a capped ≤0.5% position size.

top_movers.catalyst: No company-specific news on 2026-05-29; sector-driven lift — European defense re-armament tailwinds continued to carry CAC 40 aerospace names, with Safran recovering from a 17% 3-month drawdown toward analyst consensus of ~347 EUR.
top_movers.miss_reason: Outside any active strategy's universe. Safran is a CAC 40 mega-cap (~€133B) in French aerospace/defense, likely too large and low-beta for the bot's universe; the +1.58% reflected broad sector drift rather than a priceable stock-specific catalyst.
top_movers.suggestion: Add a STOXX Europe Aerospace & Defense sector-momentum sleeve for large-caps (SAF, HO, AIR) triggered when the sector 5-day z-score ≥1.0 to capture steady defense-spending drift moves without requiring a stock-level event.

top_movers.catalyst: Week-long rebound from May low (~1,053p) persists, sustained by CEO Erginbilgiç's May 25 Bloomberg interview on turnaround milestones and UBS raising Power Systems growth target from 20% to 26%; no single fresh intraday driver on May 29.
top_movers.miss_reason: Outside any active strategy's universe — RR.L is a FTSE 100 mega-cap aerospace/defence/industrial conglomerate. The move was a multi-day CEO-interview continuation, not an intraday gap-up, providing no news-reactive or momentum entry signal.
top_movers.suggestion: Add FTSE 100 aerospace/defence names to universe with a 'media-catalyst rebound' filter: enter if stock closes ≥3 consecutive up days off a 20-day low within 5 sessions of a CEO major-media event.

top_movers.catalyst: Stock went ex-dividend for €1.00 annual dividend on 2026-05-29 (~3.5% mechanical adjustment); actual -2.08% close-to-close reflects partial recovery from underlying buying on AGM day.
top_movers.miss_reason: Outside any active strategy's universe. DBK.DE is a large-cap European financials name (Deutsche Bank, Europe's 8th-largest bank by assets); the move is a mechanical ex-dividend drop, not a tradeable momentum or mean-reversion signal.
top_movers.suggestion: Add an ex-dividend calendar screen to the daily 'biggest losers' watchlist scan so mechanical ex-div drops are flagged and auto-excluded from analyst review queues, reducing false-positive noise.

top_movers.catalyst: No fresh news today — continuation of multi-day technical correction; 50-day MA (€167) breached May 28 after Q2 EPS matched but revenue missed by 1.9%, with trailing P/E at 67x vs 38x sector avg driving sustained profit-taking.
top_movers.miss_reason: Outside any active strategy's universe. ENR.DE is a DAX large-cap industrial/energy-transition name; today's move is a multi-session fade with no intraday trigger, offering no actionable entry signal for momentum or news-reactive systems lacking this ticker in their defined universe.
top_movers.suggestion: Add a 'valuation-extended DAX large-cap' sub-universe: names with YTD gain >30% and trailing P/E >55x; enable mean-reversion short entry when price closes below 50-day MA on ≥1.3× avg volume — this setup was present at the May 28 open.

top_movers.catalyst: No identifiable catalyst; stock reversed prior session's +1.68% gain in profit-taking after the May 11 H1 earnings rally; AI-impact-on-office-catering overhang continues to cap sentiment.
top_movers.miss_reason: Outside any active strategy's universe — FTSE 100 mega-cap (~£55bn) food-services name. Drift without a hard catalyst won't trigger momentum or news-reactive signals, and mean-reversion edge is minimal on this large-float, low-velocity name.
top_movers.suggestion: (No actionable change — CPG.L's mega-cap scale and food-services sector fall outside current risk tolerance; capturing this drift would require a new large-cap FTSE 100 mean-reversion universe layer, not a parameter tweak.)

top_movers.catalyst: Post-Q1 earnings miss residual sell-off (EPS $1.27 vs $1.38 est; rev $4.52B vs $4.68B est, reported May 15) augmented by UK Tobacco and Vapes Act 2026 regulatory overhang; no new catalyst today.
top_movers.miss_reason: Outside any active strategy's universe — a mega-cap FTSE 100 tobacco name (beta 0.46, ~£100B mkt cap). The triggering Q1 miss fired two weeks ago; today's drift is slow-burn post-earnings repositioning with no fresh entry signal for momentum or news-reactive strategies.
top_movers.suggestion: Add a post-earnings residual-drift gate: FTSE 100 names with EPS miss ≥5% and no rebound within 8 sessions stay eligible for a short tilt, sized at beta × base_size to cap exposure on low-volatility defensives.
